correctness.notes: Multiple internal contradictions. REQ-T1-1 says 'exactly the following 5 scenarios' then lists 4 and has a correction note saying 'freeze as 4' — broken REQ structure; item #5 is a meta-note, not a scenario path. REQ-T1-2 inline note says 'pipeline-history-pii-scope and pipeline-paused-webhook moved to EXTEND for REQ-T1-3' but REQ-T1-3 EXTEND list DOES NOT contain either — 2 orphan scenarios. Arithmetic error: spec claims 'RETIRE=4 (total = 39 classified + 2 reference/template = 41)' but only 1 reference template (needs-clarification-e2e.sh) is named, and 13+14+8+4=39; +1 template=40, not 41. The 2 orphans are the missing piece but the spec instead invokes an unnamed '2 reference/template' to cover the gap. Gate-1 override mandated 'ALL doc-grep REWRITE candidates from Phase 2 inventory' = 14; Phase 2 inventory lists 14 DOC_GREP REWRITE entries INCLUDING pipeline-paused-webhook. REQ-T1-2 substitutes pipeline-history-append (HYBRID, was REWRITE in inventory) and drops pipeline-paused-webhook/pipeline-history-pii-scope — arbitrary departure from user override. AWK_SOURCE_PATTERN regex in design §4.3 is byzantine and likely buggy under bash single-quoted interpretation (double-escapes of parentheses and braces may not match real awk invocations). REQ-T1-9 punts harness count (186 vs traceability-computed 204) to Phase 5 — REQs should be frozen at Phase 4.

completeness.notes: Holes: (a) v6.9.0-pipeline-history-pii-scope.sh and v6.9.0-pipeline-paused-webhook.sh have NO classification in any REQ (REWRITE/EXTEND/RETIRE/KEEP) — orphaned despite explicit inline-note claim they're in EXTEND. (b) design §8.4 lists only 2 net-new scenarios while traceability.md §Net-new enumerates 19 — MAJOR inconsistency between companion docs. (c) anti-pattern harness gate uses hardcoded TOUCHED_SCENARIOS array (design §4.2) — NO automated mechanism to keep array synced with REQ-T1-1/T1-2/T1-3 lists; drift risk unaddressed. (d) spec does NOT specify what happens if operator installs validate-dispatch.sh hook but no .ceos-agents/ directory exists for the current invocation — design §3.2 covers missing state.json but not missing parent directory (implicit pass, but not called out). (e) tests/lib/fixtures.sh require_jq behavior when scenario forgets to set FIXTURES_REQUIRE_JQ but relies on jq is not covered — silent HAVE_JQ=0 could cause false-pass. (f) Traceability references 'tests/scenarios/v6.10.0-fixtures-helpers-contract.sh' for REQ-T1-4/17/18 but this scenario is not listed in design §8.4 new-files manifest nor mentioned in design §5 step 4. (g) Commit ordering (per MEMORY.md feedback_docs_coverage discipline) IS specified in design §6 (20 steps, good). (h) 11 Track 3 agents fully enumerated (REQ-T3-3, good). (i) 14 REWRITE targets fully enumerated (REQ-T1-2, good but under-counts per Phase 2). (j) Track 2 pipeline-agent-dispatch-models.sh:92 grep update specified surgically (REQ-T2-1, good).

security.notes: Residual risks explicitly cited with deferral slots: T3-ADV-1/2/3 → REQ-T3-12 + roadmap v6.11.0 Prompt-injection defense-in-depth (AC-T3-12-2). T2-ADV-3 (Autopilot × hook) → REQ-T2-9 + Out-of-Scope §5.1 #3 → v6.10.1. No ghost references. Design §3.3 security contract is solid: no $(...), no eval, no backticks, jq 2>/dev/null everywhere, hardcoded STAGES whitelist, exit-0-always, plain-text log (no jq in log read path). AC-T2-4-2 gates forbidden patterns. No implicit command-injection sinks in design.md — jq filters use --argjson (safe) not string interpolation. Minor: make_state_json accepts arbitrary JSON fragment via --argjson; malformed JSON fails at jq parse time (safe), not via shell. NFR-7 (no global config writes) covered by AC-T2-6-2. Minor gap: REQ-T2-FALLBACK handles LOW/MEDIUM confidence from REQ-T2-8, but does NOT cover the case where research returns HIGH confidence but the API is materially different from design.md's assumption (exit codes, invocation contract). Prompt question (b) specifically flagged this — no explicit reconciliation step between research and design before Phase 5 begins coding.

maintainability.notes: REQ IDs stable (REQ-T1-N, REQ-T2-N, REQ-T3-N, REQ-META-N with sequential numbering). 77 ACs mapped, ratio 1.6 within target. But: REQ-T1-1 has broken structure (item #5 is a correction note, not a scenario); REQ-T1-9 self-admits 'will be re-frozen in Phase 5' — REQs should be frozen at Phase 4. REQ-T2-FALLBACK uses non-numeric suffix (mildly inconsistent with T2-1..T2-13 pattern). No over-long REQs (>100 words) observed. Traceability orphan check claims 0 orphans but the 2 missing scenarios (pipeline-history-pii-scope, pipeline-paused-webhook) ARE orphan — check is vacuous. Design §8.4 listing 2 net-new vs traceability §Net-new listing 19 is a serious companion-doc consistency issue that violates NFR-9 'No orphan REQs, no orphan ACs' in spirit. Commit ordering and cross-track dependencies well-documented (design §6).

robustness.notes: Edge cases partially covered. (a) If anti-pattern gate fails on one specific file after all 14 REWRITEs finish — no rollback path specified; spec assumes Phase 5 iterates fixer-reviewer and re-runs gate. Acceptable but implicit. (b) If REQ-T2-8 returns HIGH confidence but API differs from design.md assumption: REQ-T2-FALLBACK only triggers on LOW/MEDIUM — no reconciliation step for HIGH-but-different. (c) Phase 7 execution ordering enforced in design §6 (20 steps, correct order T1-RETIRE → T2-prereq → T1-DSL → T2-Layer1 → ...). Good. (d) design §4.3 awk+source regex: AWK_SOURCE_PATTERN with deep escape sequences inside bash single-quotes is brittle; negative control AC-T1-7-3 only validates one synthetic pattern — real-world awk+source lifts with different awk syntax (e.g., awk '/pattern/,/pattern/') may slip. (e) make_state_json with empty jq (return 2) — caller may not check return code, silently produces empty $STATE variable; design §2.2 acknowledges 'Caller decides policy' but fixtures.sh should hard-fail when used incorrectly. (f) Cross-run behavior of setup_scratch's trap: calling setup_scratch multiple times overwrites SCRATCH but only the last trap fires EXIT cleanup — design §2.1 comment claims 'idempotent by key' but the implementation (trap 'rm -rf "$SCRATCH"' EXIT) is NOT key-idempotent; it replaces prior trap. Bug.

phase_3_decisions_coverage.decision_2_track_1_rewrite_scope: COVERED_with_user_override_noted (REQ-T1-2 uses 14 per Gate 1 override; brainstorm Decision #2 said 5)
phase_3_decisions_coverage.decision_4_track_2_dispatched_at_check: COVERED (REQ-T2-4, AC-T2-4-3, state/schema.md additive per REQ-T2-5)
phase_3_decisions_coverage.decision_6_phase_4_external_research: COVERED (REQ-T2-8, REQ-T2-9, REQ-T2-FALLBACK; traceability references .forge/phase-4-spec/research/*.md but those files are not yet delivered — a separate Phase 4 track)

top_findings.one_liner: Two scenarios (v6.9.0-pipeline-history-pii-scope.sh, v6.9.0-pipeline-paused-webhook.sh) are orphaned — REQ-T1-2 note says they moved to EXTEND, but REQ-T1-3 EXTEND list does NOT contain them; traceability orphan check is vacuous.
REQ-T1-1 lists 4 scenarios but header says 'exactly the following 5'; item #5 is a correction note, not a scenario path. REQ is structurally broken and self-contradicts.

design.md §8.4 lists only 2 net-new test scenarios; traceability.md §Net-new enumerates 19. Either design §8.4 is incomplete or traceability over-counts — the two cannot both be true at Phase 5 hand-off.

REQ-T1-9 harness count (186) contradicts traceability-computed count (204 = 185 baseline + 19 net-new); spec self-admits this will be 're-frozen in Phase 5', violating the Phase-4-is-authoritative discipline.

Anti-pattern harness gate (design §4.2) uses hardcoded TOUCHED_SCENARIOS bash array with no sync mechanism against REQ-T1-1/T1-2/T1-3; also setup_scratch trap is NOT idempotent despite design §2.1 comment claim (trap 'rm -rf "$SCRATCH"' EXIT replaces prior trap).

design §4.3 AWK_SOURCE_PATTERN regex with deep escape sequences inside bash single-quoted string is brittle; negative-control AC-T1-7-3 only validates one synthetic pattern — real-world awk variants (e.g., awk range-matching) may evade detection.

REQ-T2-FALLBACK only triggers on LOW/MEDIUM confidence from REQ-T2-8; no reconciliation path specified if research returns HIGH confidence but API contract materially differs from design.md assumption (exit codes, invocation contract, trigger scope).

recommendations: BLOCK: Fix REQ-T1-1 structure (header says 5, body has 4). Choose one and update correction note to match.

BLOCK: Reclassify v6.9.0-pipeline-history-pii-scope.sh and v6.9.0-pipeline-paused-webhook.sh explicitly (add to EXTEND, add to REWRITE as 15th/16th, or add to RETIRE). Phase 2 inventory has them as REWRITE. The user override mandated REWRITE=14 (ALL doc-grep REWRITE candidates) but spec freezes a 14-entry list that excludes these 2 — either the scope was shrunk silently or they need to go into EXTEND with explicit paths.
BLOCK: Reconcile design.md §8.4 and traceability.md §Net-new — either design §8.4 should enumerate all 19 net-new scenarios, or traceability §Net-new list is over-scoped.
FIX: Freeze REQ-T1-9 harness count at the correct integer (likely 204 per traceability, assuming all 19 net-new ship). Remove the 'will be re-frozen in Phase 5' escape hatch.
FIX: Replace design §4.3 hardcoded TOUCHED_SCENARIOS with `find` + classification-tag heuristic, OR add an AC that asserts array contents exactly match the REQ-T1-1/T1-2/T1-3 lists at release time.
FIX: Correct design §2.1 setup_scratch to be truly idempotent (append to trap or use stacked-trap pattern), or drop the 'idempotent by key' comment.
CONSIDER: Add reconciliation-step REQ for Phase 5 — 'before writing hook, diff research findings against design §3 assumptions; if material differences, update design + re-run Gate 2 review'.
pass_fail_summary: FAIL — 2 HARD and 1 MEDIUM correctness+completeness issues block Phase 5 readiness. Security and maintainability are acceptable; correctness and completeness sub-scores fall below the 3-of-5 minimums, and weighted overall 2.7 < 3.5 threshold.
